text: When the default mode network is active, it is not passive. Neuroimaging studies show it coordinates the hippocampus and the medial temporal lobe in a process researchers call "memory consolidation by replay." During wakeful rest, the hippocampus re-activates recent experiences in compressed, accelerated form — sometimes hundreds of times per minute — forwarding them to cortical storage. This is why a student who rests after studying retains significantly more than one who immediately encounters new material. Cognitive psychologist Lila Davachi at NYU calls this "awake replay" the brain's filing process: it is not wasted time, it is the sorting of everything time contains.
question: According to the passage, what does the hippocampus do during wakeful rest?

options.text: It suppresses recent memories to prevent interference with future learning.
It re-activates recent experiences in compressed form and forwards them to cortical storage.
It generates novel connections between unrelated memories.
It synchronizes with the prefrontal cortex to evaluate emotional significance.
